----- PAGE 1 -----
 
CITIZEN’S CHARTER 
FY 2026 EDITION  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
----- PAGE 2 -----
 
ii 
 
AGENCY PROFILE 
 
 
I. MANDATE 
 
Section 2 of Republic Act No. 8468 or “An Act Converting the Don Severino Agricultural 
College in the Municipality of Indang, Province of Cavite into a State University, to be known 
as the Cavite State University” states that,  
 
“The University shall primarily provide advance instruction and professional training in 
agriculture, science and technology, education and other related fields, undertake research 
and extension services, and provide progressive leadership in these areas.”  
 
 
II. VISION 
 
The premier university in historic Cavite globally recognized for excellence in character 
development, academics, research, innovation and sustainable community engagement..  
 
 
III. MISSION 
 
Cavite State University shall provide excellent, equitable and relevant educational 
opportunities in the arts, sciences and technology through quality instruction and responsive 
research and development activities. It shall produce professional, skilled and morally upright 
individuals for global competitiveness.   
 
IV. SERVICE PLEDGE  
 
We Commit to the highest standards of education, value our stakeholders, Strive for continual 
improvement of our products and services, and Uphold the University’s tenets of Truth, 
Excellence, and Service to produce globally competitive and morally upright individuals.

1. Action on Contracts and External Agreements
This procedure aims to ensure that complete staff work (CSW) is properly observed prior to the approval 
of the University President. 
Office or Division: Office of the University President 
Classification: Simple 
Type of 
Transaction: 
G2C – Government to Citizen 
G2G – Government to Government 
G2B – Government to Business Entity 
Who may avail: All 
CHECKLIST OF REQUIREMENTS WHERE TO SECURE 
Draft MOA/proposal with appropriate/related 
attachments to support the document. End-user/proponent 
CLIENT STEPS AGENCY ACTION FEES TO BE 
PAID 
PROCESSING 
TIME 
PERSON 
RESPONSIBLE 
1. Submit the physical
document or
scanned copy if
through email for
review of the
President.
1.1. Acknowledge receipt of 
the document. None 1 Minute OP Staff 
1.2. Review the document as 
to content and 
attachments. 
None 2 Minutes OP Staff 
2. Receive the
document or
acknowledge
properly the receipt
of the copy if it is via
email and to comply
with all the needed
attachments.
2.1. If found incomplete, return 
to the end-user/origin.  None 1 Minute OP Staff 
2.2. If the document is clear 
and complete, encode the 
details to the computer for 
recording and easy 
tracking. 
None 1 Minute OP Staff 
2.3. Forward the document to 
the President. None 1 Minute OP Staff 
2.4. Review/comment on the 
document None 1 Day University 
President 
2.5. Once reviewed, encode 
the comments on the 
logbook for proper 
documentation. 
None 1 Minute OP Staff 
2.6. Submit the MOA for 
review and legal 
comments of the legal 
Department (if needed) 
via personal or online.  
None 1 Minute OP Staff 
3
----- PAGE 53 -----
3. Receive the
document by
indicating in the
logbook the
name,signature and
date received, or
acknowledge
properly the receipt
of the copy it is via
email.
3.1. Release to the end-
user/origin and have them 
sign in the logbook or 
provide them a copy and 
ask them to acknowledge 
once received if it is via 
email.

2. Signing of Student Clearance

Type of

TIME

1. The patient to come
in or brought in as
emergency case.

immediately.

Duty (NOD)

comfortable position and 
ask for patient’s ID for 
proper identification and 
for recording purposes. 
a. If patient is
unconscious and
pulseless. 
1.a.1. Administer First Aid
treatment and call other 
medical staff for 
assistance. 
None 
10 Minutes / 
depending on 
the case 
NOD / 
Physician

medical history (if
accident in 
nature) and

assessment of 
vital signs.

or incidence report,

record vital signs. None 5 Minutes NOD

physician on duty.

30 Minutes/

1. Procedure on Investigation
This procedure aims to enumerate proper investigation of one individual, university personnel and student 
to gather information in the scene of the crime. 
Office or Division: Civil Security Services Office (CSSO) 
Classification: Simple 
Type of 
Transaction: G2G – Government to Government 
Who may avail: All students, University Personnel and outsider 
CHECKLIST OF REQUIREMENTS WHERE TO SECURE 
Incident Report Civil Security Services Office 
CLIENT STEPS AGENCY ACTION FEES TO BE 
PAID 
PROCESSING 
TIME 
PERSON 
RESPONSIBLE 
1. Report the incident
to the Guard on
Duty
1.1. Report the incident to the 
Chief Security Officer / 
Security Investigator 
None *1 Hour 30
Minutes
Security 
Guard on 
Duty & 
Security 
Investigator 
1.2. Question the person of 
interest and gather 
relevant data to analyze 
the situation. 
1.2.1. If the person of 
interest is a 
student, the 
incident must be 
reported to the 
Office of Student 
affairs for 
appropriate action. 
1.2.2. If the person of 
interest is an 
employee, it must 
be reported to the 
HRDO for proper 
appropriate action. 
1.3. Within 24 hours, cases 
involving outsiders should 
be handled in 
coordination with the local 
PNP and the barangay 
concerned. 
1.4. Take a photo 
documentation of the 
situation (if possible) 
None 5 Minutes Security 
Investigator 
1.5. Make a report of the 
incident that occurred and 
submit it to the office 
concerned. 
None 30 Minutes Security 
Investigator

7. Procedure on Risk Management
This procedure aims to prepare and secure the University community including visitors, thus ensuring 
safety during emergencies inside the campus. 
Office or Division: Civil Security Services Office (CSSO) 
Classification: Simple 
Type of 
Transaction: G2C-Government to Citizen 
Who may avail: All students, University Personnel and Visitors 
CHECKLIST OF REQUIREMENTS WHERE TO SECURE 
Integrated of Disaster Risk Reduction Education into 
School Curricula and Program and Mandatory Training 
for the Public Sectors Employee. 
Local Government Unit’s Office 
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan Civil Security Services Office (CSSO) 
Primer on Campus Security Civil Security Services Office (CSSO) 
CLIENT STEPS AGENCY ACTION FEES TO BE 
PAID 
PROCESSING 
TIME 
PERSON 
RESPONSIBLE 
• Earthquake Emergency
A. If an emergency occurs at
Cavite State University,
the security personnel or
safety building officer
leads the sound of the
alarm.  Instructions are
immediate disseminated to
the University personnel
and students to vacate the
affected building and
facilities.
B. The security personnel or
safety building officer shall
give instruction to form
SINGLE FILE AND 
PROCEED TO THE 
NEAREST EXIT DOOR at 
THE RIGHT SIDE OF THE 
PASSAGEWAYS. 
Moreover, the left side 
should a cleared for the 
responding emergency 
teams. Everyone shall 
proceed and use the right 
way of the working area in 
None 
Within the 
time of 
emergency 
Security 
Personnel 
and safety 
building 
officer 
244
----- PAGE 294 -----
leaving the area.  The floor 
diagrams installed at the 
doors and lobbies show 
the location of exits and 
stairways. FIND THE EXIT 
WAY BEFORE AN 
EMERGENCY OCCURS. 
C. The security personnel
and or safety building
officer shall instruct the
university employees and
students to vacate the said
building and proceed to
the designated assembly
open area in front of the
building to await further
instructions. Security
personnel will check the
building to ensure that all
personnel have
successfully evacuated.
➢ DO NOT RE-ENTER
THE BUILDING
UNLESS IT IS
DECLARED SAFE.
➢ DO NOT ATTEMPT TO
REMOVE YOUR CAR
FROM THE
PREMISES, THIS WILL
ONLY HINDER
EMERGENCY
VEHICLES.
➢ DO NOT GO HOME
UNTIL NOTIFIED
THAT YOU MAY DO
SO.
The security personnel 
shall keep all records or 
reports of the events inside 
the university campus 
245
----- PAGE 295 -----
• Fire Emergency
A. If the security personnel
or anybody discovers fire:
• Shout FIRE and
activate the nearest
manual alarm.
• Dial the nearest fire
station in the locality
and give exact location
of the fire.
• If it is safe, fight the fire
using portable fire
extinguisher or water
reel, otherwise,
evacuate the area at
once.
If heavy smoke is 
present, everyone must 
keep its head low about 
12 inches above the floor 
but not below 4 inches 
where toxic gases may 
accumulate. If time 
permits, they may wrap a 
towel or cloth, blankets 
or linen around their 
head and face to provide 
some protection from 
heat and smoke. 
Randomly opening a 
very hot door may cause 
air to be sucked in and 
cause a back -draft 
explosion, which may 
escalate the fire. 
DO NOT ATTEMPT TO 
FIGHT A FIRE UNLESS 
YOU ARE: 
None 
Within the 
time of 
emergency 
Security 
Personnel 
and safety 
building 
officer 
246
----- PAGE 296 -----
• Familiar with the 
operating procedures 
for fire extinguisher. 
• Near an exit so that you
can escape quickly; and
• Far from any dangerous
materials, such as
gasoline, paint thinners,
and LPG.
The security personnel 
or the guard on duty 
must make sure to get 
everybody out of the 
workplace before 
attempting to extinguish 
the fire.  He must keep 
the escape route at his 
back and get out if the 
fire can no longer be 
controlled. Close the 
door to imp ede the 
spread of the flame and 
remove burned materials 
away from other 
flammable items for it 
may cause another fire. 
Lastly, do not panic to 
avoid stampede. 
• Packages Bomb Threats
Bomb threats usually 
occur during times of 
unrest or unusual 
tension. They may be 
made by criminal or 
mischievous elements. It 
is advisable for everyone 
to be prepared. The 
following are the 
recommended 
precautionary measures: 
• Guards and lobby 
receptionists are 
instructed not to receive 
mail packages on 
None Around a 
minute 
Security 
Personnel on 
duty 
247
----- PAGE 297 -----
behalf of CvSU 
personnel. 
• Beware of unusual, oily
or heavy packages.
• Look out for wires
sticking out of any
package received.
• Beware of any pungent
or unusual odor.
Look out for suspicious 
markings. 
• Telephone Threats
In dealing with telephone 
bomb threats, a standard 
procedure is designated 
to avoid the person in 
line from panicking. If 
you have received a 
telephone bomb threat, 
follow the procedures 
given. Keep a copy close 
to your telephone. Call 
the security immediately 
after the call and submit 
the form. The Security  
Office will investigate the 
call, assess the situation, 
carry out a search and 
decide if evacuation is 
necessary. 
The following are the 
prescribed procedures 
when receiving a 
telephone bomb threat. 
• Keep caller on the
phone.
• Voice intonations
(sounds like a foreigner,
Visayan, Ilocano,
Ilonggo, etc.)
• Observe for 
background noises 
(sounds like rooster,  
None Within that 
time of calling 
Security 
personnel on 
duty 
248
----- PAGE 298 -----
dogs, vehicle motor 
engines, etc.) 
• Ask caller the purpose
of doing this, try to
bother his conscience.
• Ask caller where the
bomb is planted  /
located.
• Ask caller the kind of
bomb planted.
• Ask caller from where
he is calling.
• Ask caller the time
when bomb is set to
explode.
• After the conversation,
immediately inform
CvSU authorities /
Building Administrator
about the threat.
• Announce through
public address system
the threat.
• Ask assistance from
security.
• Call PNP Bomb
Disposal Unit in case a
bomb is located.
Evacuation in the Event of a 
Bomb Threat 
If it has been decided 
that there is a need to 
evacuate the building, 
an announcement will 
be made over the public 
address system and 
alarms will be sounded. 
They must follow 
building evacuation 
procedures in a calm 
and orderly manner. 
And wait for further  
instructions in the 
assembly areas. 
TOTAL: None

2. Application for Designated Faculty Extensionist 
This procedure aims to define the selection process for Designated Faculty Extensionist (DFE). 
Office or Division: Extension Services Center (ESC) 
Classification: Highly Technical 
Type of 
Transaction: G2G - Government to Government

CHECKLIST OF REQUIREMENTS WHERE TO SECURE

Can be downloaded at the CvSU website 
1. Updated curriculum vitae 
2. Copy of Office/Individual Performance 
Commitment and Review (O/IPCR) for the last two 
rating periods 
3. Copy of approved proposal of the extension 
program/project implemented as program/project 
leader 
4. Copy of notarized Memorandum of Agreement 
(MOA) with partner agencies for the extension 
program/project 
5. Copy of terminal report of the extension 
program/project implemented as program/project 
leader. 
To be provided by the applicant 
CLIENT STEPS AGENCY ACTION FEES TO BE 
PAID

TIME

u.ph 
1.1. The ESC, through its

and will forward them to

(MED). 
None *8 Days 
ESC Staff/ 
MED? ESC 
Director/ 
VPRIE/ OUP 
1.2. MED conducts an initial 
screening of the

Extensionist Routing Slip 
(EXTN-QF-15) to check if 
the applicant has met the 
minimum qualifications 
and to determine the 
completeness and 
accuracy of the

supporting documents 
submitted. If the applicant 
288
----- PAGE 338 -----
 
did not meet the minimum 
qualifications, he/she will 
be immediately informed. 
In case the submitted 
documentary 
requirements are 
incomplete, the applicant 
will be requested to 
submit the necessary 
supporting documents. 
1.3. The Director of the ESC 
shall evaluate applicants 
based using the Criteria 
for Evaluation of DFE 
(EXTN-QF-34). 
Applications that meet the 
criteria shall be endorsed 
to the Vice President for 
Research, Innovation, 
and Extension (VPRIE) 
for further endorsement 
and approval by the 
University President. In 
cases of disapproval, the 
MED will notify the 
applicant. 
1.4. The University President 
approves/ disapproves of 
the designation of the 
faculty extensionist. If 
approved, MED will 
facilitate the signing and 
release of a Special Order 
designating the 
successful applicant as 
faculty extensionist. If 
disapproved, the MED will 
notify the applicant. 
2. Successful DFE 
applicants will 
report to the 
Director of the 
Extension Services 
Center. 
2.1. The MED will orient the 
newly designated DFE 
and facilitate the 
finalization and approval 
of the plan of activities 
and discuss the 
monitoring and evaluation 
mechanism.

MED

extension 
program/project,

approved program/

extension programs/projects. 
Office or Division: Extension Services Center (ESC) 
Classification: Highly Technical 
Type of

year program project, and

program/project

of an existing extension

date. The letter should be

within the specified 
timeframe may result in 
the denial of the 
extension request. 
1.10. The project 
management team with 
completed or terminated 
extension 
programs/projects must 
submit a Terminal Report 
(EXTN-QF-06) within one 
month of completion or 
termination.

7. Coffee Processing Center (CPC) 
This procedure aims to have a standard procedure in the production of Aguinaldo Blend Coffee.

Transaction:

G2B – Government to Business Entity

PAID

TIME 
PERSON 
RESPONSIBLE 
1. Sorting of green 
coffee beans 1.1. The staff sort by removing 
defects and I purities from 
good beans. 
None 
Depending 
on the 
number of 
defects in 
green coffee 
beans 
CPC In-
charge/Staff

assessment form 
for verification. 
2.1. The staff separate good 
and bad beans. 
None 
Depending 
on how many 
good beans 
are going to 
be put in the 
jute sack 
CPC In-
charge 
2.2. The staff dispose of the 
bad beans and put the 
good beans in a jute sack 
and store them in the 
designated stockroom. 
3. Blending of three 
varieties of green 
coffee beans 
3.1. The in-charge blends 
according to the specified 
Aguinaldo blend ratio. 
None 3 Minutes CPC In-
charge 
4. Weighing of green 
coffee beans 
4.1. The in-charge weighs 
10kgs of green coffee 
beans for each batch of 
roasting. 
None 3 Minutes CPC In-
charge 
5. Roasting of green 
coffee beans 5.1. The in-charge set the 
roasting machine to a 
specified temperature, 
different minutes for the 
1st batch and 2nd batch 
and succeeding batches. 
None 
30 minutes 
for 1st batch, 
25 minutes 
for 2nd and 
succeeding 
batches 
CPC In-
charge 
6. Cooling of roasted 
coffee beans 6.1. The in-charge put the 
roasted beans in the 
cooling chamber. 
None 5 Minutes CPC In-
charge 
454
----- PAGE 504 -----

roasted beans 7.1. The in-charge set aside 
the roasted bean before 
grinding. 
None 24 Hours CPC In-
charge

beans according to the 
specified texture. 
None 2 Minutes per 
kilo 
CPC In-
charge

coffee beans

1. Processing of Incoming Communication Related to Events 
This service covers the receipt, logging, review, routing, and response to all event-related 
communications addressed to the Events Management Office, including letters, invitations, 
endorsements, coordination requests, and similar documents from internal and external stakeholders. 
Office or Division: Events Management Office (EMO) 
Classification: Simple 
Type of 
Transaction: 
G2G – Government to Government 
G2B – Government to Business 
G2C – Government to Citizen 
Who may avail: CvSU Offices, Units, Colleges, Campuses, and External Agencies/Clients

Official Letter / Invitation / Request for Coordination Client / Sending Office

communications coursed through higher offices) 
Endorsing Office (e.g., OUP, OVPBRG, VP 
Offices) 
Supporting documents (e.g., program, concept note, 
proposal) Client / Sending Office

PAID 
PROCESSING 
TIME 
PERSON 
RESPONSIBLE 
1. Submit 
communication 
(letter, invitation, 
request) to EMO via

1.1. Receives, logs, and 
acknowledges the 
communication in the 
EMO registry. None 1 Day EMO Staff 
1.2. Assigns tracking or 
reference number, if 
applicable. 
2. Provide 
clarifications or 
additional 
documents when 
requested by EMO. 
2.1. Reviews contents and 
determines appropriate 
action (for noting, for 
coordination, for 
endorsement, or for 
scheduling). 
None 2 Days Head, EMO; 
EMO Staff 
2.2. If necessary, requests 
additional information or 
documents from the 
sender. 
2.3. Routes the 
communication to the 
Head, EMO for guidance, 
notation, or decision. 
3. Await EMO 
response or 
feedback. 
3.1. Prepares draft reply 
(letter, email, or memo) 
indicating EMO’s action, 
recommendation, or 
schedule. 
None 3 Days Head, EMO; 
EMO Staff

1. Request for Event Management Assistance 
This service covers requests from external agencies, organizations, and private clients for EMO 
coordination, technical supervision, and related services (including income-generating services such as 
coordination packages, LED wall and equipment use, and documentation). External clients may be 
endorsed by University offices or may directly coordinate with EMO, subject to approval and compliance 
with University policies. 
Office or Division: Events Management Office (EMO) 
Classification: Simple 
Type of 
Transaction: 
G2G – Government to Government 
G2B – Government to Business 
G2C – Government to Citizen 
Who may avail: External government agencies, LGUs, NGOs, private organizations, media 
partners, and individuals with approved use of CvSU facilities

activities Client

Working Committees or manpower assignments (if any) Client / Partner Office

1. Request for Event Management Assistance 
This service covers requests from CvSU offices, units, colleges, and campuses for assistance in the 
planning, coordination, on-ground supervision, and post-event evaluation of internal activities. Working 
committees are provided by the host college/unit or requesting office, while EMO provides professional 
guidance, technical oversight, and protocol and branding compliance. 
Office or Division: Events Management Office (EMO) 
Classification: Simple 
Type of 
Transaction: G2G – Government to Government 
Who may avail: CvSU Offices, Units, Colleges, Campuses

This procedure shows how emergency care and treatment are provided to patients in the University.

Classification: Simple

1. The patient to come

recording purposes.

Duty (NOD)

for assistance.

*10 Minutes

medical history (if 
accident in nature)

signs.

or incidence report,

record vital signs.

physician on duty.

process.  
2.1. Administer intensive

done to the immediate 
head/guardian.

*30 Minutes 
depending on 
the case  
Physician

1. Health Monitoring of All CvSU Personnel and Visitors Upon Entry to University 
This procedure aims to monitor the present health status of all CvSU personnel and visitors who 
enter the university campus during pandemic occurrence. This includes health survey, contact 
tracing, and safety precautions.  
Office or Division: Health Services Unit 
Classification: Simple 
Type of 
Transaction: G2C – Government to Client 
Who may avail: CvSU Personnel and Visitors 
CHECKLIST OF REQUIREMENTS WHERE TO SECURE 
UHSE-QF-17 Health Declaration Form University Health Services Unit 
CLIENT STEPS AGENCY ACTION FEES TO BE 
PAID 
PROCESSING 
TIME 
PERSON 
RESPONSIBLE 
1. Fills-out Health 
Declaration Form 
and submits it to 
the Guard on Duty.  
  
1.1. Provides the copy of 
health declaration form to 
the Security Unit.  
None 1 Minute Nurse on 
Duty 
1.2. Distributes copy of health 
declaration form to every 
CvSU personnel and 
visitors that enter the 
University.  
None 1 Minute Security 
Guard 
1.3. Checks the answers in 
the health survey portion 
of the Health Declaration 
Form.  
None 1 Minute Security 
Guard 
1.4. If the answer in the health 
survey portions are all NO 
and the personnel or 
visitor had no fever, 
he/she will be allowed to 
enter the campus.  
None 1 Minute Security 
Guard 
1.5. If there is one or more 
YES answer/s with or 
without fever, the security 
personnel will refer and 
send him/her to the 
University Health 
Services Unit for further 
evaluation, management 
and advises.  
1.6. Returns back the Health 
Declaration Form to 
Health Services Unit.  
None 1 Minute Security 
Guard

Investigator

3. Procedure on Investigation

outsiders to initially gather information on reported incidents.

Type of 
Transaction: G2C – Government to Citizen 
Who may avail: All students, university employees and outsider  
CHECKLIST OF REQUIREMENTS WHERE TO SECURE 
Incident Report Concerned Individual 
CLIENT STEPS AGENCY ACTION FEES TO BE 
PAID 
PROCESSING 
TIME 
PERSON 
RESPONSIBLE 
1. Report the incident 
to the Guard on 
Duty.   
  
1.1. Report the incident to the 
Chief Security Officer / 
Security Investigator.  
None 30 Minutes 
Security 
Guard on 
Duty 
1.2. Question the person of 
interest and gather 
relevant data to analyze 
the situation.  
1.3. If the person of interest is 
a student, the incident 
must be reported to the 
Office of Student affairs 
for appropriate action.  
1.4. If the person of interest is 
an employee, it must be 
reported to the HRDO for 
proper appropriate action 
within 24 hours, cases 
involving outsiders should 
be handled in 
coordination with the local 
PNP and the barangay 
concerned.   
None 1 Hour Security 
Investigator 
1.5. Take a photo 
documentation of the 
situation (if possible).  
None 5 Minutes Security 
Investigator 
1.6. Make a report of the 
incident that occurred and 
submit it to the office 
concerned.  
None 1 Hour Security 
Investigator

1. Program Offered Inquiries 
This procedure outlines the systematic process for receiving, addressing, and documenting inquiries 
related to the academic programs offered by the department. It ensures that students, parents, and 
external stakeholders receive accurate, timely, and consistent information regarding admission 
requirements, curriculum details, program outcomes, and other academic-related concerns.  
Office or Division: Teacher Education Department 
Classification: Simple 
Type of 
Transaction: G2G – Government to Government 
Who may avail: Students, Faculty, and Graduate Students 
CHECKLIST OF REQUIREMENTS WHERE TO SECURE 
Program Offered Checklist and Description Department Chairperson 
Valid ID Student / Graduate Students 
Accomplished Inquiry Form Student / Graduate Students 
CLIENT STEPS AGENCY ACTION FEES TO BE 
PAID 
PROCESSING 
TIME 
PERSON 
RESPONSIBLE 
1. Present a valid ID 
and submit a 
completed Inquiry 
Form indicating the 
program of interest 
and your contact 
details. 
  
1.1. Receive and 
acknowledge inquiries 
about programs offered 
through walk-in visits, 
phone calls, emails, or 
online platforms. 
None 10 Minutes Program 
Coordinator 
1.2. Assess and categorize 
the inquiry to determine 
the specific program or 
information requested.  
None 10 Minutes Program 
Coordinator 
1.3. Verify and gather 
accurate information from 
official department 
records, program 
brochures, or designated 
faculty/program 
coordinators.  
None 5 Minutes Program 
Coordinator 
1.4. Provide an official 
response to the inquirer, 
ensuring clarity, 
completeness, and 
alignment with 
institutional guidelines.  
None 5 Minutes Program 
Coordinator 
1.5. Document and record the 
inquiry and the 
corresponding response 
in the departmental log for 
reference, monitoring, 
and reporting purposes.  
None 10 Minutes Program 
Coordinator

TIME

RESPONSIBLE

and submit a

Form indicating the

and your contact 
details.

preliminary evaluation of 
the submitted 
requirements to confirm 
completeness and 
compliance.

Charge

appropriate faculty 
member for official review 
and authorization.  
None 10 Minutes Faculty-in-
Charge

completion information in 
the departmental registry 
and prepare the official 
completion report.

Charge

grade or completion 
document to the 
requesting student.

Charge

2. Borrowing of Equipment 
This procedure establishes the guidelines for the borrowing, monitoring, and return of equipment within 
the department. It aims to ensure that all equipment is requested properly, issued responsibly, recorded 
accurately, and returned in good condition to maintain inventory integrity and support academic and 
operational needs.  
Office or Division: Teacher Education Department 
Classification: Simple 
Type of 
Transaction: G2G – Government to Government 
Who may avail: Faculty Members and Students

Valid ID Borrower 
CLIENT STEPS AGENCY ACTION FEES TO BE 
PAID 
PROCESSING 
TIME 
PERSON 
RESPONSIBLE

Borrowing Form 
and present valid 
ID.

equipment borrowing 
request form to verify 
completeness and 
eligibility of the borrower. 
None 5 Minutes Faculty-in-
Charge 
1.2. Check equipment 
availability and assess the 
condition and functionality 
of the requested item(s).  
None 5 Minutes Faculty-in-
Charge 
1.3. Record and document the 
approved request in the 
equipment logbook or 
inventory system for 
tracking and 
accountability.  
None 5 Minutes Faculty-in-
Charge 
1.4. Release the equipment to 
the authorized borrower, 
ensuring proper 
acknowledgment through 
signature or digital 
confirmation.  
None 5 Minutes Faculty-in-
Charge 
1.5. Monitor and enforce 
return schedules, 
including inspection of the 
equipment upon return 
and updating the 
inventory records 
accordingly.

1. Consultation and Referrals 
This procedure outlines how the Campus Health Services Unit supports the assessment of a patient's 
physical condition and the administration of treatment. 
Office or Division: Campus Health Services Unit 
Classification: Simple 
Type of 
Transaction: G2C-Government to Citizen 
Who may avail: Students, Faculty Members and Employees

to the nearest hospital.

2. Provision of Emergency Care and Treatment 
This procedure aims to provide a systematic method and immediate actions for a patient who needs 
urgent emergency care and treatment. 
Office or Division: Campus Health Services Unit 
Classification: Simple 
Type of 
Transaction: G2C-Government to Citizen 
Who may avail: Students, CvSU Faculty Members, Employees, and Campus Officials  
CHECKLIST OF REQUIREMENTS WHERE TO SECURE 
Consultation Form Patient/ Client 
Request Form 
Campus Health Services Unit 
Monitoring Sheet 
Referral Form 
Medical Certificate Form 
Prescription Form 
Request Form 
Laboratory Result Form 
X-Ray/ Diagnostic Result Form 
CLIENT STEPS AGENCY ACTION FEES TO BE 
PAID 
PROCESSING 
TIME 
PERSON 
RESPONSIBLE 
1. The patient comes 
in or is brought in 
as an emergency 
case 
1.1 Places the patient in a 
comfortable position. 
1.2 Provides necessary 
assistance/treatment 
1.3 Secures the patient’s 
identity 
None 
 
 
2 Minutes 
 
 
 
 
Nurse on Duty 
 
 
1.a. Unconscious 
and pulseless: 
1.a.1. Administer First 
Aid treatment, call 
other medical staff for 
assistance, and assess 
if needed to transfer to 
the nearest hospital 
None 
10 Minutes/ 
depending 
on the case 
Nurse on Duty 
or if Physician 
Available 
1.b. If conscious, the 
patient gives 
medical history  
(if accident in 
nature) and 
submits to 
assessment of 
vital signs 
1.b.1. Elicits medical 
history or incident 
report/s, checks, 
monitors and records 
vital signs 
 
1.b.2. Refers the 
patient to the physician 
on duty if applicable or 
transfers to the nearest 
hospital 
None 
10 Minutes/ 
depending 
on the case 
Nurse on Duty

2. The patient shows 
improvement and 
stability of the 
condition  
2.1 Prescribes medication with

gives advice. May issue a 
medical certificate if

physician is available. None 3 Minutes Nurse on Duty 
/ Physician

3. Issuing of Excuse Letter 
This procedure aims to provide a letter for students who cannot attend class due to a health condition, or 
for not wearing the prescribed uniform or shoes. 
Office or Division: Campus Health Services Unit 
Classification: Simple 
Type of 
Transaction: G2C – Government to Client 
Who may avail: Students

Excuse letter from their Parents/Guardian 
Patient/ Client Medical Certificate from the Government or a Private

PAID 
PROCESSING 
TIME

RESPONSIBLE

to the Nurse on

information from the 
identification card and 
completes the consultation 
form 
None 1 Minute Nurse on Duty 
2. Assessment of vital 
signs and the 
present Medical 
Certificate of 
Excuse letter from 
their Guardian/ 
Parent 
2.1 Check and record vital 
signs 
None 1 Minute Nurse on Duty 2.2 Assesses the results for 
comments and 
recommendations 
3. For a patient who 
requests not to 
wear shoes or a 
school uniform 
3.1 Assess the severity of the 
wound or health condition 
and give approximately 4-
5 days' exemption. After 
this, the patient should 
provide a medical 
certificate if the period 
exceeds 6 days. 
None 1 Minute Nurse on Duty 
4. The student 
receives a letter, 
and the patient 
signs the logbook 
4.1 Encodes, saves, and prints 
the online documents for 
filing and safekeeping 
None 1 Minute Nurse on duty 4.2 Log the student’s name, 
address, age, sex, course, 
and contact number

4. Issuance of Certificate of Good Moral Character 
This procedure shows how to assist bonafide students and graduates who would want to secure Certificate 
of Good Moral Character 
Office or Division: Office of Student Affairs and Services 
Classification: Simple 
Type of 
Transaction:

2. Collection of Fees 
This procedure shows how the Cashier’s Office collect fees, charges and other assessments from 
Departments, Bureaus, Offices and Other Agencies.  
Office or Division: Cashier’s Office  
Classification: Simple 
Type of

other means of providing 
identity.

Duty 
2. Unconscious and 
pulseless:.

needed to transfer to the 
nearest hospital.

10 Minutes  
(depending 
on the case) 
Nurse on 
Duty/ 
Physician 
Available

medical history (if 
accident in nature)

signs.

incidence report, checks,

signs.

10 Minutes 
(depending 
on the case)

Duty 3.2. Refers patient to 
physician on duty if 
applicable or transfer to 
the nearest hospital.

improvement and

condition.

Duty

1. Preparation of Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) 
This procedure covers how the Supply Office facilitates the preparation of the Project Procurement 
Management Plan (PPMP) of the different units of the Campus. 
Office or Division: Supply Office/Budget Office 
Classification: Highly Technical 
Type of 
Transaction: G2G – Government to Government 
Who may avail: All CvSU Employees 
CHECKLIST OF REQUIREMENTS WHERE TO SECURE 
PPMP Form Supply Office 
CLIENT STEPS AGENCY ACTION FEES TO BE 
PAID 
PROCESSING 
TIME 
PERSON 
RESPONSIBLE 
1. Submit their 
accomplished 
PPMP form.  
1.1. Evaluate to ensure 
consistency with the 
budget proposal and in 
compliance with the 
existing budget rules. 
None 20 Minutes Budget Staff 
1.2. Consolidate PPMPs.  None 15 Days Supply Staff 
1.3. Submit Indicative PPMP 
to the HOPE for approval 
of the oversight body.  
None 10 Minutes Supply Staff 
1.4. Submit approved PPMP 
to the CvSU- Main 
campus procurement 
office for consolidation.  
None 10 Minutes Supply Staff 
2. Submit Revised 
PPMP.  
2.1. Consolidate revised 
PPMP.  None 15 Days Supply Staff 
2.2. Submit Final APP to the 
HOPE for approval of the 
oversight body.  
None 10 Minutes Supply Staff 
2.3. Provide end-users copy of 
the approved PPMP.  None 10 Minutes Supply Staff 
 
2.4. Provide client with the 
Client Satisfaction 
Measurement Survey 
Form or the link and/or 
QR Code below. 
https://forms.gle/smYUwU
7Ly5RGaKwQ9

Office

Transaction:

CHECKLIST OF REQUIREMENTS WHERE TO SECURE

Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable 
Properties (IIRUP) Supply Office 
Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Semi-
expendable Properties (IIRUSP) Supply Office 
Tally Out Sheet Bidder 
CLIENT STEPS AGENCY ACTION FEES TO BE 
PAID 
PROCESSING 
TIME 
PERSON 
RESPONSIBLE 
1. Return
unserviceable
properties together
with Property
Return Slip.
1.1. Verify entries to the 
Property Return Slip if 
listing conforms to the 
actual returned items. 
None 5 Minutes Supply Staff 
1.2.  Accept the returned 
item/s and prepare IIRUP 
/ IIRUSP 
None 10 Minutes Supply Staff 
1.3. Verify with the Accounting 
Office the depreciation 
and net book value of the 
item/s.  
None 10 Minutes Supply Staff 
1.4. Submit the IIRUP / 
IIRUSP to the Disposal 
Committee.  
None 5 Minutes Supply Staff 
1.5. Deliberate on the 
submitted listings and 
inspect the items to be 
disposed.  
None 1 Day Disposal 
Committee 
1.6. Finalize IIRUP/ IIRUSP, 
prepare other supporting 
documents and submit to 
the Disposal Committee. 
None 1 Day Supply Staff

campus at a given place and time for  purpose of gathering evidence and conducting an investigation.  
Office or Division: Physical Plant and Security Services (Campus Security and Safety unit) 
Classification: Simple 
Type of

Who may avail: Employees, Students, Stall Owners

information about the event and the identification Card of 
the requester

PAID 
PROCESSING 
TIME 
PERSON 
RESPONSIBLE

(Security service),

out the CCTV Security 
service logbook.

Duty 
2. Fill out the Access
request form &
other pertinent
documents (To be
filled out only by the

the form and other 
pertinent documents 
needed. None 2 Minutes

Campus 
Security 
Officer 
(watchman)

information to the

personnel, and

investigation.
3.1. Check the completed 
form submitted by the 
requester. 
None

exceeding 
Fifteen (15)

Days 
PPSS Staff /

PPSS 
Personnel

period to comply with 
such a request may be 
extended for an additional 
period not exceeding 
fifteen (15) working days,

with prior notice to the 
requesting party.

footage/s needed as 
evidence.

Type of 
Transaction: 
G-2-C Government to citizen

Who may avail: All students, university personnel, and outsiders

Incident Report Physical Plant and Security Services  
CLIENT STEPS AGENCY ACTION FEES TO BE 
PAID 
PROCESSING 
TIME 
PERSON 
RESPONSIBLE 
1. Report the incident 
to the guard on duty 
1.1 Report the incident to the 
Chief Security Officer / 
Security Investigator  
None 30 Minutes to 
1 Hour 
Security 
Guard on 
Duty 
1.2 Question the person of 
interest and gather 
relevant data to analyze 
the situation.  
 
1.2.1. If the person of 
interest is a student, 
the incident must be 
reported to the Office 
of Student affairs for 
appropriate action.  
 
1.2.2. If the person of 
interest is an 
employee, it must be 
reported to the HRDO 
for proper appropriate 
action.  
1.3 Within 24 hours, cases 
involving outsiders should 
be handled in coordination 
with the local PNP and the 
barangay concerned.  
1.4 Take a photo 
documentation of the 
situation (if possible)  
None 5 Minutes

incident that occurred and 
submit it to the office 
concerned

Client Satisfaction 
Measurement Survey

1. Selection and Hiring for Academic Positions 
This procedure aims to ensure that selection and hiring of academic personnel are made in accordance 
with CvSU Guidelines, CSC Revised Omnibus Rules on Appointment and Other Human Resource 
Actions (ORA-OHRA) and DBM Circulars.  
Office or Division: Campus Human Resource Development Office  
Classification: Simple 
Type of 
Transaction: G2C – Government to Citizen 
Who may avail: All Qualified Applicants for the vacant academic position    
CHECKLIST OF REQUIREMENTS WHERE TO SECURE 
Applicant Interview Rating Sheet  Campus Human Resource Development 
Office  
Oral Interview Evaluation Score Sheet  Campus Human Resource Development 
Office  
Evaluation Checklist for Demo Teaching – for Academic 
Personnel only  
Campus Human Resource Development 
Office  
Written Examination  Campus Human Resource Development 
Office  
CLIENT STEPS AGENCY ACTION FEES TO BE 
PAID 
PROCESSING 
TIME 
PERSON 
RESPONSIBLE 
1. Report to the 
campus for written 
examination, 
teaching demo and 
interview.   
1.1. Notify the applicant via 
phone call, email or text 
message regarding the 
schedule of evaluation.  
None 1 Minute 
 
Campus 
HRD 
Coordinator / 
Assistant 
 
1.2. Gather the local 
Personnel Selection 
Board for evaluation of 
the applicant.   
None 5 Minutes 
 
Campus 
HRD 
Coordinator / 
Assistant 
 
 
1.3. Evaluate the applicant for 
demo teaching and 
through panel interview.  None 30 Minutes 
 
Campus 
Personnel 
Selection 
Board 
Committee 
 
1.4.  Advise that the applicant 
will be notified on the 
status of application.     
None 2 Minutes 
Campus 
HRD 
Coordinator 
 
1.5. Tally and summarize the 
evaluation ratings of 
applicant.  
None 30 Minutes 
Campus 
HRD 
Coordinator / 
Assistant 
2. Receive notification 
on the status of 
application.  
 
2.1. Inform the applicant 
through phone call, text 
message, or email of the 
result of evaluation and of 
None 3 Minutes 
Campus 
HRD 
Coordinator

requirements.  
2.2. Endorse the

2. Processing of Appointment of Job Order Academic Personnel (Campus)  
This procedure aims to ensure that processing of appointments of University personnel are made in 
accordance with CvSU Guidelines, CSC Revised Omnibus Rules on Appointment and Other Human 
Resource Actions (ORA-OHRA) and DBM Circulars.  
Office or Division: Campus HRDO - Recruitment, Selection and Placement (RSP) Unit  
Classification: Simple 
Type of 
Transaction: G2C – Government to Citizen 
Who may avail: All selected applicants  
CHECKLIST OF REQUIREMENTS WHERE TO SECURE 
Personal Data Sheet (CS Form No. 212)  www.csc.gov.ph or CSC website  
Personal Description Form (DBM-CSC Form No.  10)  www.csc.gov.ph or CSC website  
Medical Certificate (CS Form No. 211)  University Infirmary  
TOR, Diploma, eligibility, PSA Birth Certificate and NBI 
Clearance, TIN  Concerned Agencies  
Certificate of first day of service  Office of the Campus Administrator  
Community Tax Certificate  Barangay/Municipal/City Registrar’s Office  
CLIENT STEPS AGENCY ACTION FEES TO BE 
PAID 
PROCESSING 
TIME 
PERSON 
RESPONSIBLE 
1. Submit all required 
documents for 
appointment on Job 
Order status.

• PDS, PDF; 
• TOR/Diploma; 
• Medical 
Certificate;  
• NBI Clearance;  
• Other pertinent 
documents; and  
• Evaluation 
Result  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
1.1. Receive the submitted 
requirements of the Job 
Order academic 
personnel.  
None  1 Minute  HR 
Coordinator 
1.2. Review and validate the 
submitted documents for 
completeness and 
accuracy.  
None 5 Minutes 
 
HR

photocopied documents 
for authentication to the

the Liaison Officer.   
None  5 Minutes  
HRDO 
Coordinator/ 
Liaison 
Officer 
1.4. Prepare the contract of 
service to be signed by 
the Job Order Academic 
Personnel.  
None 3 Minutes HR 
Coordinator 
 
1.5. Read and sign the 
contract of service.  None 3 Minutes 
Job Order 
Academic 
Personnel 
 
1.6. Verify and endorse the 
signed contract of service 
to the Campus 
Administrator for 
signature.  
  
None  
  
  
  
  
3 Minutes  
  
  
  
HR

Classification: Simple 
Type of 
Transaction: G2C – Government to Citizen 
Who may avail: All Active and Employees who are no longer connected with the University

CLIENT STEPS AGENCY ACTION FEES TO BE 
PAID 
PROCESSING 
TIME 
PERSON 
RESPONSIBLE 
1. Fill out the Record 
Request form and 
submits to the HR 
Coordinator.   
1.1. Receive and review the 
duly accomplished form 
for the completeness of 
entries.  
None 3 Minutes 
HR 
Coordinator/ 
Staff 
1.2. Check and verify from file 
the requested record. If 
requested record 
/document is not available 
in the campus, request 
will be endorsed to the 
University HRDO.   
None 10 Minutes 
HR 
Coordinator/ 
Liaison 
Officer

documents.  
None 5 Minutes HRDO 
personnel

Classification: Simple 
Type of 
Transaction: G2C – Government to Citizen

program 
CHECKLIST OF REQUIREMENTS WHERE TO SECURE 
Duly accomplished Application for Admission Form Registrar’s Office / University Website 
Copy of Report Card / Transcript of Records Previous School Attended 
Certificate of Good Moral Character Previous School Attended 
2 pcs. 2x2 Recent ID Picture Applicant 
Valid ID (for transferees) Applicant 
CLIENT STEPS AGENCY ACTION FEES TO BE 
PAID 
PROCESSING 
TIME 
PERSON 
RESPONSIBLE 
1. Submit the 
application form and 
required documents 
to the DIT Office.  
 
1.1. Verify completeness of 
documents. None 2 Minutes 
Admission 
Staff/ DIT 
Secretary 
 
1.2. Schedule an interview and 
inform the applicant. None 10 Minutes 
Admission 
Staff/ DIT 
Secretary 
2. Attend the interview. 
 
2.1. Conduct an interview. None  10 Minutes Department 
Chair 
3. Wait for the result. 
 
3.1. Notify the applicant of the 
result (or via email or 
posting). 
None 2 Minutes DIT 
Secretary 
 
3.2. Provide client with the 
Client Satisfaction 
Measurement Survey 
Form or the link and/or

Members

Members

requirements if 
notified.

submission is incomplete 
or incorrect and specify 
what must be revised or 
completed.

Members

in the class record,

CLIENT STEPS AGENCY ACTION FEES TO BE 
PAID 
PROCESSING 
TIME 
PERSON 
RESPONSIBLE 
1. Submit 
accomplished 
clearance form.   
1.1. Receive the clearance 
and verify the 
completeness of the 
requirements.  
None 2 Minutes TED Faculty 
Members 
2. Wait for review and 
feedback.  
2.1. Notify the student to 
provide the lacking items, 
if documents are 
incomplete. 
None 3 Minutes TED Faculty 
Members 
3. Resubmit missing 
requirements, if any.  
3.1. Re-check and confirm that 
all requirements are now 
complete. 
None 3 Minutes TED Faculty 
Members 
4. Receive signed 
clearance.  
4.1. Sign the clearance once 
all requirements are 
properly verified and 
complied with.

Chairperson

1. Review and Evaluation of Research Proposal for Research Grants 
This service covers the formal assessment of research proposals submitted by faculty members or 
students for institutional or external funding. The review evaluates the proposal’s relevance, feasibility, 
methodology, ethical considerations, expected outputs, and budget justification to ensure alignment with 
the institution’s research agenda and funding agency requirements. Only proposals meeting the 
prescribed standards are endorsed for grant application or approval. 
Office or Division: Research Services Unit 
Classification: Simple 
Type of 
Transaction: G2G – Government to Government 
Who may avail: Faculty researchers and students applying for research grants 
CHECKLIST OF REQUIREMENTS WHERE TO SECURE 
Full Research Proposal  Researcher

This service involves the formal assessment of students’ research manuscripts to ensure compliance

4. Validation of Research Instrument 
This service includes the expert review and validation of research instruments such as questionnaires, 
interview guides, or testing tools. The validation ensures that the instrument is clear, relevant, aligned 
with research objectives, and appropriate for data gathering. Recommendations for revisions and 
improvements are also provided. 
Office or Division: Research Services Unit 
Classification: Simple

Office or Division: Campus Health Services Unit

TIME

1. The patient to come 
in or brought in as

recording purposes.

a. Unconscious 
and pulseless: 
a. Administer First Aid 
treatment and call other 
medical staff for 
assistance 
None 
10 Minutes* 
  
(depending 
on the case) 
NOD / 
Physician

patient gives 
medical history 
(if accident in 
nature) and 
submit to 
assessment of 
vital signs.

incidence report,

record vital signs.  
 
b.2 Refer patient to 
physician on duty.

consultation 
process.  
2.1. Administer intensive

necessary steps to be 
done to the immediate 
head/guardian.  
None 
30 Minutes* 
(depending 
on the case)  
Physician

until he/she becomes

these books..

book’s or books’ 
barcodes. Collects the 
library fine* (accumulated 
library fine is computed 
per day - from day 1 of 
overdue to day of return). 
Inserts the book cards 
then shelves these books. 
 
*Including Saturdays, 
Sundays and holidays

change without prior 
notice

1972
----- PAGE 2022 -----
 
FEEDBACK AND COMPLAINTS MECHANISM 
HOW TO SEND FEEDBACK Accomplish Stakeholders’ Feedback Form and drop it at the 
Suggestion Box in front of the frontline desk officer. 
HOW FEEDBACKS ARE 
PROCESSED 
The Unit Head collects, evaluates and consolidates the 
accomplished form on a weekly basis. 
 
Stakeholder feedback analysis will be presented to all staff 
during weekly and quarterly meetings. 
 
The Unit Head submits a copy of report of stakeholders’ 
feedback thru summarized reports.  
 
The HR staff consolidates all the reports of stakeholders’ 
feedback from all units/colleges. 
HOW TO FILE A 
COMPLAINT 
Proceed to HR office and accomplish Stakeholder’s Complaint 
Form. 
 
Complaints may be also received thru phone and e-mail. 
HOW COMPLAINTS ARE 
PROCESSED 
The HRDO staff assesses the situation/complaint and reports it 
to the HR Director. 
 
In the case that the complainant is present, the HR Director will 
try to resolve as quickly as possible the complaints which are 
straightforward and require little or no investigation.  
 
For complaints received by phone and in e-mail, the HR 
Director solicits facts and other information from the 
employee/unit being complained to fully understand the 
situation. The process has a maximum duration of five (5) 
working days upon receipt of the complaint. 
 
The HRDO communicates the frontline resolution outcome to 
the complainant. This may be done face-to-face, by phone, in 
writing or e-mail. 
The concerned agency endorses the complaint letter coursed through 8888/Contact Center ng 
Bayan. 
1973
----- PAGE 2023 -----
 
Within 72 hours, the University, through the Office of the University President (OUP) receives 
the complaint letter and endorses the same to the Office of the Vice President for Administrative 
and Support Services (OVPASS) for assessment. 
 
Likewise, the concerned unit/individual is furnished by OUP with the copy of the complaint letter 
and instructed to submit a draft response to the OVPASS.  
 
The assigned Alternate Focal Person for the Main and Branch campuses verify the letter reply of 
the concerned unit and submit a final draft to the OVPASS for approval. 
 
The approved letter reply is then endorsed by the OVPASS to the OUP for signature of the 
University President and submission to the CCB/8888 via moroceo@ched.gov.ph; 
ched4a@ched.gov.ph; jmachan@ched.gov.ph; 8888hotline@ched.gov.ph; lgueta@ched.gov.ph 
and dbuenaagua@ched.gov.ph.  
CvSU-HRDO:      
 
MAIN CAMPUS 
(046)483 9250 local 1017 
(046) 419-4527 
cvsuhrd@cvsu.edu.ph 
 
CCB: 0908-881-6565 / 8888 
 
PCC: (02)-8736-8645 / (02)-8736-8603
